title: Level 380
wikitext:
{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=hotelbedroom.png|class=Undetermined|hexagon=?|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Presence of Hostile Entities|text-color=#f3f3f3|bg-size=1600px}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 380: Untitled Hotel}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Google+Sans:ital,opsz,wght@0,17..18,400..700;1,17..18,400..700&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: Google Sans}}
[[File:hotelcorridor.png|2000px]]
[[File:Vignette.png|2000px]]

{{Background|file=hotelcorridor.png}} {{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color: #2D2D36; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #3F3F4A; --theme-page-text-color: #EAEAF3; --theme-community-header-color: #EFEFF4; --theme-link-color: #ACACD4; }} {{Quote|The near-endless staircase, the immaculate state of the hotel, and the shadowed cityscape, it . . . it reminds me of home. After all the levels I’ve escaped, after enduring horrors beyond comprehension, this place almost erases them, like the faint ashes of old scars, leaving me isolated in a realm of eerie, yet familiar comfort. To whoever manages to find this . . . please, cherish the final moments of your fragile life. It’s no longer worth fighting for anything.}} '''Level 380''', also known as "Untitled Hotel," is the 381st level of the Backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=hotelroomer.png|caption=The first known image of the dining rooms.}} '''Level 380''' is a hotel complex maintained in a state of perpetual, pristine condition, its architecture defined by an infinite rectangular-spiral staircase that serves as the central connective structure between its uniformly pristine floors. Each level contains a series of dining chambers outfitted with rotating centre tables and neatly arranged utensil sets, including stainless steel sporks, spoons, and wooden chopsticks, all exhibiting no signs of wear or contamination. The walls are adorned with decorative panels featuring abstract, geometric patterns embedded seamlessly into the surface, their purpose ambiguous but their presence consistent across all documented rooms. Illumination is provided by elongated fluorescent fixtures mounted on the ceiling, which emit a steady, low-frequency hum similar to that of [[Level 0]], but is much fainter to the human ear. Select chambers feature large, floor-to-ceiling windows framed by immaculate white curtains, though these serve no apparent functional purpose beyond aesthetic consistency. {{Blurimage|align=left|file=newhoteloutside.png|caption=A photo of exterior structures visible from '''Level 380's''' windows.}} ‍﻿‌‎⁠ Exterior observation through these windows reveals a sprawling urban region composed of crumbling brick high-rises, their exterior consisting of rusted barbed wire, exposed ventilation ducts, and a labyrinth of overgrown electrical cables. A consistent day-night cycle has been documented, with periods of dim, overcast daylight transitioning into near-total darkness, though no celestial bodies or natural light sources are identifiable. Closer inspection of these structures during daylight phases occasionally yields glimpses of discarded objects within their darkened interiors; fragments of aluminium cans, disposable lighters, and tightly knotted plastic bags of indeterminate contents, though the opacity of the shadows obscures further detail. Despite extensive testing, the windows have proven resistant to all known methods of breaching, rendering physical exploration of the external environment impossible. As such, the structural integrity, purpose, and potential hazards of these buildings remain widely unknown, with current data limited to visual analysis alone. The sterility of '''Level 380’s''' interior stands in stark contrast to the decay beyond its borders, raising speculations of the level being composed of by two colliding environments, in which some theorize that this is a failed rendering, a level caught between iterations.The validity of this claim remains unverified, as the external structures do not clearly correspond with any previously documented levels. Some speculate that they may represent deteriorated or abandoned sections of [[Level 11]], though this hypothesis has not been fully recognised. {{Blurimage|file=Hotelbedroom.png|caption=A hotel room with pink bedding and a study desk.}} The dormitory units of '''Level 380''' present as sparse residential accommodations, markedly less frequent than dining facilities and exhibiting significantly higher rates of restricted access. Each unit features illuminated numeric identification above its entryway, with interior corridors characterized by white plaster walls, ceramic tile flooring, and irregularly spaced fluorescent lighting. Periodic safety installations include non-functional fire-extinguishers and misleading exit signs that typically terminates in non-viable spaces. The existence of elevators has been confirmed, though they remain entirely non-functional as all control buttons are unresponsive. Any attempts to block the doors or manipulate materials within the level have proven infeasible with existing technology. Standard room configurations contain a centrally positioned queen-sized sleeping platform with random lateral orientation, accompanied by compact work surfaces stocked with standard hospitality items including writing implements and personal care products, though notably lacking sanitary facilities like bathrooms. Occasional wall-mounted artwork appears above bedding platforms, while looking through windows reveals views of adjacent dormitory structures exhibiting the same modern architectural design of the level, suggesting other settlements of such hotels beyond its exterior. This oddly contrasts with depictions inside dining rooms, as they showcase the previously mentioned deserted and decaying high-rises outside their windows instead of other hotel structures. Upon close observation of bedding materials, they reveal persistent textile wrinkling and trace aromas combining lavender and ash compounds, suggesting undocumented occupation patterns within these spaces. {{Blurimage|file=Hotelcorridor.png|caption=The hotel’s internal passageway system, exhibiting its typical features.}} The central staircase constitutes '''Level 380's''' primary architectural feature and most common entry vector, though alternative noclipping manifestations occur in dining seating or dormitory sleeping platforms. Construction features include grey woollen carpeting on stepped surfaces and glass safety barriers with wooden handrails. Sub-step fluorescent lighting provides minimal illumination, with intensity decreasing proportionally to the level's depth. At irregular intervals, the staircase’s geometry undergoes a strange reconfiguration; landings fold into walls, steps compress into non-Euclidian shapes, and entire walls invert into one another, usually hindering further travel. These changes are said to occur only in peripheral vision, and resets instantly when observed directly.The irregular wall structures are likely hallucinations originating from deeper sections of the level. What remains unexplained is how these purely perceptual phenomena can physically hinder progress, as wanderers report encountering tangible resistance from structures that should not objectively exist. Beyond approximately 200 meters of travel using stairs, ambient lighting undergoes progressive failure, resulting in monochromatic visual perception, characterised by a dull grey ambience seen through both human vision and camera lens, along with a significant loss of sanity within 2-4 hours of exposure. Upon noclipping or teleporting into the level for the first time, electrical infrastructure such as light sources demonstrates initial functionality but predictably fails within 6-8 hours of occupation, coinciding with emergence of hostile entities. Hence, this egress route remains the only documented survival option, necessitating standard rations and resources including Almond Water provisions and illumination equipment for safe navigation. {{Blurimage|align=left|file=Electricalroomnew.png|caption=A photo of the electrical room.}} Notably, few documented cases describe wanderers being abruptly relocated to specific zones of the level, particularly the electrical room and deteriorated hotel sections. The electrical room presents exposed wiring, either severed or dangling, creating significant electrocution hazards. Power banks within this space can be manipulated via unlabelled switches, allowing limited control over localized power distribution, though trial-and-error remains the sole method of determining their function. Another documented teleportation zone comprises the level's deteriorated sectors, where the hotel's structure has collapsed entirely. Here, mattresses lie gutted, furniture stands overturned, and curtains hang in tatters. Dining rooms in these sectors display shattered dishware, scattered cutlery, and destabilized lighting fixtures suspended from the ceiling, usually landing above tables and damaging it. While the cause of this remains unconfirmed, the predominant theory attributes these modifications to prolonged occupation by Hounds and Smilers.
[[File:cityscapee.png|400px|link=]]
{{Quote|The Backrooms twist reality—a place where lies feel like home. Among them stands a hotel, simple at first glance, but hiding something deeper. Its true nature only shows itself when you go too far, when you push past where the level wants you to stop. Down there, in the spaces it tries to hide, you realize some truths aren’t meant to be found.}}
Deeper Into The Hotel
I don’t know how long I’ve been climbing. The staircase never ended, just step after step, landing after landing, all identical, all looping in ways that made no sense. But today . . . today, I found a door. It shouldn’t have been there. None of the doors in that place ever opened, but this one did. And when I stepped through, I wasn’t in the staircase anymore. Cold air hit me first. Then the sound, distant, humming, like a city at night. I blinked hard, trying to clear my vision, but everything stayed blurred, smeared like wet ink. Tall shapes loomed in the haze, jagged and towering. Skyscrapers? Their windows were lit, glowing squares in the fog, but no matter how I strained, I couldn’t focus. I tried to walk forward, but my legs wouldn’t move right. It was like the ground tilted under me, like the air itself pushed back. The harder I fought to see, the dizzier I got, and then, the worst part, I realized it wasn’t me. The level didn’t want me here. I don’t know what’s down there. Maybe it’s the center. Maybe it’s something worse. But the staircase isn’t just infinite. It’s alive, and it doesn’t let go of what’s its. I’m back on the steps now. The door’s gone. The lights are gone. But sometimes, when I stop to rest, I hear the hum of a city far below, and I wonder if I’m still climbing down . . . or if it’s climbing up to meet me.
''— Unknown''
''October 14th, 2003''
Entities
The environment of '''Level 380''' sustains persistent populations of [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 2|Windows]], and an anomalous entity by the name of "The Receptionist", each demonstrating behavioural and morphological adaptations specific to the level's architecture, in which most is not properly understood. To date, no confirmed contact between wanderers has been documented, given the level's seemingly infinite scale. Smilers emerge exclusively during light-source failure events, with numbers increasing dramatically in proportion to extended time periods in the level. Their physical structure deviates from baseline documentation, consistently presenting six dorsal limbs used for vertical traversal and stair descent. Approximately a quarter of observed specimens also exhibit a bulkier structure, though its sensory or evolutionary purpose remains unconfirmed. Hounds occupy dining areas in ambush posture, primarily concealed beneath tables or under dormitory beds. Size variations indicate either prolonged exposure to the environment or multiple distinct subspecies, with larger specimens demonstrating increased aggression thresholds. Notably, limb deformation correlates with size, as specimens exceeding a few meters in length universally present irregular joint structures. Aside from the hostile entities, Windows function as non-interactive environmental fixtures. Visual analysis confirms their projection of artificial landscapes, primarily warm-toned cityscapes and sunset simulations, across all exterior-facing panes. These projections maintain parallax consistency with observer movement before dissolving upon close approach. No physical interaction or threat response has been recorded, classifying them as atmospheric elements rather than hostile entities. ===The Receptionist=== {{Blurimage|file=receptionist.png|caption=The main reception of Level 380.}} The receptionist is a passive entity that occupies the level, appearing only as a faint, indistinct shadow near the main reception desk. The area features an oak wood desk with scattered stationery items; pens, notepads, and randomly placed chairs, along with a black swivel chair that seems meant for the entity's use. Behind the desk stands an empty wooden shelf that exhibits a peculiar anomaly, in which any object placed upon it immediately disappears the instant the observer looks away, vanishing completely even if the glance lasts merely a second. Adjacent to the primary desk is a second desk, lighter in colour, positioned at a slight angle. A second swivel chair, similar in design but fitted with beige padding, is placed behind it, though the purpose of this chair and desk remains largely unknown. When wanderers approach, they may find a pen and notebook on the desk, untouched. Writing a room number allows them to obtain corresponding keys for temporary lodging. However, if anything other than a number is written, the receptionist immediately takes the note, tears out the page, and returns the notebook to its original state. During the entity's absence, any ink written in the notebook gradually fades as if being absorbed by the paper. The receptionist is rarely present during daylight hours, appearing most frequently at night, though occasional daytime manifestations do occur. Its exact nature and purpose remain largely unknown, though it stands single as the only helpful and non-aggressive entity in the level.
Colonies and Outposts
No established colonies or outposts exist within '''Level 380'''. The level's defining architectural duality of clinically preserved interiors abruptly giving way to decayed structures outside creates inherent instability for permanent settlement. The unpredictable spatial shifts between these two states disrupt the level's structure, while the Smilers' emergence in dark areas and the large population of Hounds make consistent security protocols impossible to maintain. The level's lack of renewable resources and potable liquids further prevents sustainable occupation, though curiously, the pristine hotel interiors do temporarily support basic biological needs. All recorded settlement attempts have been abandoned within hours of habitation due to either structural collapse during boundary fluctuations or Smiler and Hound interruptions.
Entrances and Exits
===Entrances=== *Entering the boiler room in [[Level 5]] has a chance of noclipping one in the electrical room. *Attempting to sleep in room 380 of [[Level 399|Level 399's]] Rosatin Hotel may transport one here on a bed, though it is not recommended to. *Traversing through 380 of [[Level 187|Level 187's]] floors is guaranteed to lead one here. ===Exits=== *
█████ yourself is the only known exit.
Really? Are you truly this pitiful? Searching for an exit that was never meant for you? Oh, it will come, of course. One day. On the day your flesh finally fails, when your body rots and withers, seeping into these walls . . . feeding what grows beneath them. Of course, I'm just joking. There is '''one''' exit here. But the thing is, you'd probably be dead before you can even reach it. And remember how I've told you there was an exit? How you'll never be able to find it? You probably forgot by now . . . Ah, that's right. You're stuck in a loop, aren't you? You've already read that part of my message, but you came back to figure out what's going on. It's time . . . to question your own sanity. How long have you really been staying here for? There's no way to tell the time here, and even if you could, what use would that be? Even if you truly escaped this place, you'll yet again stumble across a new level, or one that you've navigated before. I'll tell you one thing. The exit you're looking for . . . [[Level 5|It's awfully familiar . . .]]
'''Footnotes'''
{{Collapse|body=
You still don’t see it, do you? Ah, there you are, wandering again. How predictable. Did you really think those doors would open for you? Look at them. Go on, press your face against the peeling paint. See how the hinges don't quite meet the frame? How they creak ever so slightly when you're not watching? I've watched every step you've taken, from the moment you stumbled into my reception, scrawling your desperate numbers on that yellowing paper, clutching those rusted keys like they meant something. You really believed it, didn't you? That this was safety. That this was peace. So you're still clinging to thoughts of escape? How ... quaint. I'll indulge your curiosity eventually, when the hour grows thin and your nerves fray just enough. Consider it a test. Of patience. Of will. Of how far you can descend these stairs, until you plunge into utter darkness, being swallowed like a fading thought. Like a fragment of a lost memory. And the lights, you’ve noticed by now, haven’t you? That faint hum isn’t the bulbs. It’s the sound of something listening. You pause, and the pause lingers a second too long. As if the silence itself is holding its breath. Oh, don’t mind the windows. They fog up sometimes. Funny, isn’t it? You’re the only one here. So why do the panes cloud over in perfect circles like mouths, sighing? And your footsteps . . . ah. You’ve heard it too. That delay. Not an echo. A reply. First matching, then . . . shifting. Slurring. As if something is tasting the rhythm of your fear. And now, the answer to your question.
 This place isn’t endless. It has exits. . . .
. . . and that's the cruelest joke of it all. You'll never find them.
[[File:deepstairs.png|400px|link=]]
